message.content:
I'm going to give you a report on how CrossFit handles penalties which is the defacto way competitions do it since they run the crossfit games. we don't want to force organizers to adhere to this system but make it clear to them given a current video submission when a performance needs to be penalized how to do that. in another branch I'm building out a review ux that creates a summary of how many no reps per movement someone got (feat/review-notes-with-movement-tagging if you want to look at the changes - don't switch branches but you can inspect). build out an adr for this (004) # CrossFit Games penalty framework: a complete system analysis

**CrossFit operates a dual-track enforcement system** — real-time "no rep" calls at live events and post-hoc video review penalties for online competitions — built around five scoring outcomes, a three-tier review escalation process, and a structured appeals mechanism. The system has undergone significant evolution, most notably in 2025 when CrossFit eliminated the minor/major penalty distinction in favor of discretionary penalties and introduced crowdsourced public video review. For anyone building competition management software, this framework reveals a complex interplay of score adjustments, percentage-based deductions, per-movement time penalties, and tiered disqualification that must account for both online and live competition contexts.

---

## The five scoring outcomes that drive everything

CrossFit's penalty architecture (codified in Section 1.22 of the Competition Rulebook) produces exactly five possible outcomes when a video or performance is reviewed. These outcomes apply primarily to online stages — the Open, Quarterfinals, and In-Affiliate Semifinals — where post-hoc video review is the enforcement mechanism.

**Good Video** means all standards were met and the submitted score stands. **Valid With Penalty** (previously split into minor and major categories) means no-reps were identified and a score adjustment is applied. **Zero Score** means egregious violations occurred — wrong movements, wrong weight, or an unacceptable number of no-reps — but the athlete remains in the competition with their other scores intact. **Invalid** is reserved for malicious intent to manipulate results, and it triggers removal of **all** scores from the competition, effectively a full disqualification.

The critical distinction between zero score and invalid matters enormously for software design: a zero score affects only one workout while invalid removes the athlete entirely from the leaderboard. A separate category — **score adjustment** — can accompany any penalty or stand alone when rep counts simply need correcting without punitive action.

Under the 2024 rulebook, minor penalties addressed "a small number of no-reps" with modest deductions, while major penalties targeted "a significant number of no-reps" with **minimum 15% deductions scaling up to 40%** of total reps or equivalent time. The 2025 rulebook collapsed these into a single "with penalty" designation, giving CrossFit full discretion over the deduction amount for each individual video. This change came directly in response to the 2024 Quarterfinals controversy where **599 penalties** were administered and the rigid percentage bands created outcomes many viewed as disproportionate.

---

## How online video review actually works

The video review process uses a **three-tier escalation system** designed to prevent individual bias. A Level 1 reviewer watches the video and flags issues. If problems are found, the video escalates to a Level 2 senior judge who independently re-reviews it. If the senior judge concurs, it moves to Level 3 — the most senior judge or team lead — who makes the final penalty determination. CrossFit's official position is that "no one judge can have too much influence on any single athlete."

Videos are **not randomly selected** for review. CrossFit prioritizes performances that affect advancement or prize money: top scores on individual workouts, athletes near qualifying cutlines, and top overall standings. During the Open, anyone who has passed the current year's Judges Course ($10, annual) can view and flag published videos on the leaderboard. For the 2025 In-Affiliate Semifinals, CrossFit formalized this into a **public review period** lasting approximately five days, where credentialed reviewers vote "Good Video," "Needs Review," or "Unviewable" on every submission. Videos accumulating significant "Needs Review" votes get escalated to CrossFit's internal team.

Video submission requirements are strict: an uncut, unedited recording from a **three-quarter angle** at minimum **3 feet off the ground**, with a visible clock throughout, no fisheye lenses, all measurements filmed, and the judge visible but not obstructing the athlete's view. For 2025 Semifinals, videos must be publicly viewable on YouTube — no private videos, no alternative platforms. Athletes requested for video review during Quarterfinals have **24 hours** to provide links; failure results in a zero score.

---

## Penalty math: time penalties, rep deductions, and percentage calculations

The specifics of penalty calculation vary by workout format. For **rep-based scores** (AMRAPs), penalties are applied as direct rep subtractions. Under the 2024 system, major penalties deducted **15–25%** of total reps depending on the number and severity of no-reps identified. For example, Pat Vellner's 252-rep score was adjusted to 202 (~20% reduction), while Tia-Clair Toomey went from 232 to 198 (~15%). Minor penalties typically reduced scores by smaller, discretionary amounts.

For **time-based scores** (for-time workouts), CrossFit applies per-no-rep time penalties that vary by movement and context. Documented penalty amounts from official workout descriptions include **10 seconds per no-rep** on snatches, **5 seconds for a false start**, and **5 seconds per instance** of receiving equipment assistance. The rulebook explicitly states that "time penalties may vary by movement and context" and reserves the right to adjust them based on workout specifics and athlete pace.

Real-world time penalty examples illustrate the range: Sam Briggs received a **2 minute 30 second penalty** on 20.1 (dropping from 8:01 to 10:31), Lefteris Theofanidis received **70 seconds** added to his 19.3 time (8:19 to 9:29), and Mikhail Anisimov received a **6-minute penalty** for using the incorrect dumbbell weight. At live Games events, successful appeals have credited athletes specific time amounts: **9 seconds**, **12 seconds**, **14 seconds**, and **30 seconds** in documented cases.

For competition management software, the key design insight is that penalty amounts are **not fixed globally** — they are set per-workout, per-movement, and sometimes per-athlete-pace, requiring a flexible penalty configuration system rather than hard-coded values.

---

## Live event judging operates on fundamentally different principles

At in-person competitions (Semifinals via In-Person Qualifying Events, and the CrossFit Games), enforcement shifts from post-hoc review to **real-time "no rep" calls**. This is the most important architectural distinction in the system: a no-rep at a live event is not a penalty — it simply means the rep doesn't count and must be re-performed immediately. No post-event score adjustment occurs for movement standard issues at live events because the floor judge handles enforcement in real time.

The judging hierarchy at the CrossFit Games places the **CrossFit Level 1 Seminar Staff** (minimum CF-L2, most CF-L3 credentialed) as floor judges for individual divisions. Team, age-group, and adaptive divisions use rigorously vetted volunteer judges with mandatory credentials: active CF-L1, current Judges Course, and prior live competition judging experience. **Head Judges** are CrossFit HQ staff who oversee entire fields of play, communicating corrections to floor judges during workouts. An **Appeals Manager** tracks appeals and communicates decisions.

Closed-circuit cameras record all primary fields of play at the Games, but this footage is used **only for appeal resolution**, not proactive post-event penalization. This is explicitly not an "instant replay" system — athletes cannot challenge calls in real time during workouts.

---

## The two-appeals rule and what can (and cannot) be challenged

At live events, athletes start with **two appeals** for the entire competition. If an appeal is granted, the athlete retains their appeal count. If denied, they lose one. Once both are exhausted, no further appeals are permitted. This creates genuine strategic stakes around when to appeal.

Appealable situations include scorecard or leaderboard errors, counting inconsistencies, load/weight recording errors, course marking mistakes, equipment failures, judge miscommunications, and interference by other athletes. Critically, **range-of-motion judgment calls are not appealable** — squat depth, hip/knee/arm extension, and overhead stabilization are considered final when called on the field.

For online competitions, the appeals process works differently. Athletes receive an email notification when scores are modified, with a brief explanation. They must contact support@crossfitgames.com with their original score, modified score, the stated reason, and their explanation for appealing. Appeals are reviewed by a **different individual** from the original reviewer. CrossFit's final decision is communicated via email and is binding.

Notable successful appeals demonstrate the system in action: Jeff Adler's double-under crossover miscounts were corrected to a new time of 6:58.32, CrossFit Oslo Kriger's recorded lift was corrected from 475 to 485 lb, and Patrick Vellner received 12 seconds credited on a sled pull penalty.

---

## Movement standards are published per-workout, not globally

Each workout releases with an official **scorecard** — a downloadable PDF containing required movements, rep schemes, equipment specifications, floor plans, and step-by-step visual movement standards with numbered photo sequences. These scorecards define what constitutes a valid rep for each specific movement in that specific workout context.

Common standard elements include hip crease passing below knee top for squats, full extension of hips/knees/arms at the top of movements, chest contact at or below the collarbone for chest-to-bar pull-ups, and both feet clearing the object during lateral burpees. The **Uncommon Movement Clause** (Section 1.18) provides a catch-all: "Any movement deemed uncommon or out of the ordinary, or used to amend, shorten, or change the accepted movement standard or range of motion... can and will be disallowed."

For Semifinals in 2025, judging requirements escalated significantly. Each athlete now needs **two judges** — a Head Judge and a Floor Judge — both holding active CF-L1 credentials, the current year's Judges Course, and the new Advanced Judges Course ($40, valid 3 years). The head judge must be introduced on camera at the start of each video. If either judge lacks required credentials, the athlete can receive a **zero score** — and this has been enforced, including against athletes at CrossFit Mayhem whose head judge had an expired credential.

---

## The 2024 penalty crisis and its systemic implications

The 2024 Individual Quarterfinals represented a breaking point for CrossFit's penalty system. **348 athletes received penalties on Workout 1 alone**, primarily for failing to fully extend hips and knees on dumbbell box step-ups — a movement that proved nearly impossible to judge consistently via video. By the end of both reviewed workouts, **599 total penalties** had been administered. **73 athletes** who were inside the qualifying Top 40 in their region were knocked outside the cut line.

The fallout was significant. Camilla Salomonsson Hellman, a four-time Games athlete, dropped from 13th in Europe to 80th overnight. Athletes described receiving penalty notifications with limited detail and no opportunity for dialogue. The Association of Fitness Judges (AFJ) noted the fundamental asymmetry: reviewing at computer speed with slow-motion capability catches standards violations that are nearly impossible to enforce at live competition pace.

CrossFit responded with structural changes for 2025: eliminating the minor/major binary, introducing the public video review system, requiring two credentialed judges for Semifinals, and partnering with the AFJ and PFAA to create themovementstandards.com — a centralized database of vetted movement standards with video demonstrations. By 2025, the penalty count actually increased to **over 3,300** across approximately 64,000 submissions (a 5%+ penalty rate), suggesting the expanded review infrastructure caught more violations rather than fewer.

---

## What this means for competition management software

Building software that models CrossFit's penalty framework requires several key architectural decisions:

- **Flexible penalty configuration per workout**: Penalty amounts (time per no-rep, percentage deductions, fixed-time penalties for procedural violations) must be configurable per workout and per movement, not globally fixed
- **Multiple penalty outcome types**: The system needs at minimum five outcome states (good, penalized, zero score, invalid/DQ, and standalone score adjustment), each with different leaderboard implications
- **Cascading score recalculation**: Penalties change raw scores, which change event placements, which change points, which change overall standings — all must recalculate automatically
- **Multi-tier review workflow**: Video review should support escalation through at least three review levels, with independent assessment at each tier
- **Appeal tracking with limited inventory**: Live events need per-athlete appeal counters that decrement on denied appeals and persist across the entire competition
- **Dual enforcement modes**: Online competitions need post-hoc penalty workflows while live events need real-time no-rep tracking integrated with scorecard entry
- **Public review integration**: Community voting on video submissions with threshold-based escalation to internal review
- **Judge credential verification**: Automated checking of judge qualifications (course completion, certification status, expiry dates) before accepting score submissions
- **Tiebreaker logic**: Intermediate tiebreak times at configurable points within workouts, with cascading tiebreak resolution across overall standings
- **Audit trail and transparency reporting**: CrossFit publishes detailed penalty reports listing every affected athlete with original scores, reasons, and adjusted scores — the software should generate equivalent reports automatically

## Conclusion

CrossFit's penalty system has evolved from ad-hoc crowd-sourced video policing (2013) into a structured three-tier review process with five defined outcomes, workout-specific penalty amounts, and formal appeal mechanisms for both online and live contexts. The 2025 shift from categorical (minor/major) to discretionary penalties and the introduction of public video review represent the most significant recent changes, driven by the 2024 crisis where rigid percentage bands produced widely criticized outcomes. The fundamental tension in the system — that video review at computer speed catches violations invisible at competition pace — remains unresolved and represents the core design challenge for any software modeling this framework. Existing competition platforms (Competition Corner, Wodify Arena, WODwin) handle registration and scoring but lack built-in penalty management, video review workflows, or appeal tracking, leaving a clear gap in the market for purpose-built competition management software.

Base directory for this skill: /Users/zacjones/Documents/02.Areas/wodsmith/thewodapp-2/.claude/skills/team-memory

# Team Memory

Manage a shared team memory system backed by a Cloudflare Worker with semantic search.

## Commands

### /remember — Store an observation

Save a new observation to team memory.

```bash
TEAM_MEMORY_URL=https://team-memory.zacjones93.workers.dev bun run .claude/skills/team-memory/scripts/remember.ts "<observation text>" [--category=<category>] [--priority=<priority>]
```

**Categories:** `convention`, `gotcha`, `debugging`, `architecture`, `workflow`
**Priorities:** `critical`, `moderate`, `ephemeral`

Defaults: category=convention, priority=moderate

Examples:
```bash
TEAM_MEMORY_URL=https://team-memory.zacjones93.workers.dev bun run .claude/skills/team-memory/scripts/remember.ts "PlanetScale returns strings for COUNT columns — always wrap with Number()" --category=gotcha --priority=critical
TEAM_MEMORY_URL=https://team-memory.zacjones93.workers.dev bun run .claude/skills/team-memory/scripts/remember.ts "Use ULID for ID generation, not CUID2" --category=convention
```

### /recall — Search memories

Search team memory by semantic query.

```bash
TEAM_MEMORY_URL=https://team-memory.zacjones93.workers.dev bun run .claude/skills/team-memory/scripts/recall.ts "<query>" [--limit=<n>] [--category=<category>] [--priority=<priority>]
```

Default limit: 5. Results are ranked by relevance.

Examples:
```bash
TEAM_MEMORY_URL=https://team-memory.zacjones93.workers.dev bun run .claude/skills/team-memory/scripts/recall.ts "database migration patterns"
TEAM_MEMORY_URL=https://team-memory.zacjones93.workers.dev bun run .claude/skills/team-memory/scripts/recall.ts "Stripe" --category=gotcha --limit=10
```

### /feedback — Rate a memory

Provide feedback on a memory's usefulness.

```bash
TEAM_MEMORY_URL=https://team-memory.zacjones93.workers.dev bun run .claude/skills/team-memory/scripts/feedback.ts <observation-id> <signal> [--note="<reason>"]
```

**Signals:** `helpful`, `harmful`, `irrelevant`

Examples:
```bash
TEAM_MEMORY_URL=https://team-memory.zacjones93.workers.dev bun run .claude/skills/team-memory/scripts/feedback.ts 01J5K3M2N7 helpful --note="Saved me from a production bug"
TEAM_MEMORY_URL=https://team-memory.zacjones93.workers.dev bun run .claude/skills/team-memory/scripts/feedback.ts 01J5K3M2N7 irrelevant
```

## Environment

Production URL: `https://team-memory.zacjones93.workers.dev`

All commands below include the env var inline. If `TEAM_MEMORY_URL` is already set in your shell, the prefix is optional.

## Hooks

- **SessionStart**: Reminds the agent about the memory system and how to use /recall for task-relevant lookups
- **SessionEnd**: Reads transcript JSONL, extracts user/assistant messages, POSTs to `/sessions`
- **Export**: Syncs memories to MEMORY.md between `<!-- BEGIN TEAM-MEMORY -->` / `<!-- END TEAM-MEMORY -->` markers


ARGUMENTS: /recall "penalty scoring review competition"

## Team Memory Results (5)

- **[01KJREPN8HHJA8MPCA5M7S3MY0]** (architecture/critical, score: 0.77, candidate)
  getPublicCompetitionDivisionsFn returns ALL levels from the scaling group, not just ones with competition_divisions rows. competition_divisions is currently an override layer, not an opt-in gate — switching a comp's scaling group exposes every division in that group to registrants.

- **[01KJRJZYM4NTQWQ6XZQ4QYAAZX]** (convention/moderate, score: 0.77, candidate)
  verifySubmissionScoreFn pattern: use requireTeamPermission(competition.organizingTeamId, TEAM_PERMISSIONS.MANAGE_COMPETITIONS) after loading competition — requireTeamPermission handles session check internally, but still call getSessionFromCookie() separately when you need session.userId for audit fields

- **[01KJ9PZR45D82NTTAF91TMRSGM]** (convention/critical, score: 0.85, candidate)
  Adding a soft-delete status column requires auditing ALL queries (heats, scores, leaderboard, results, divisions, video submission, athlete scores) — not just primary registration queries

- **[01KJTYSWMA1AYJMPT3F9MCP90E]** (architecture/critical, score: 0.84, candidate)
  Multi-tenant write endpoints: always validate the target record belongs to the actor's competition/org, not just that the record ID exists. scoreId alone is insufficient — verify competitionId too.

- **[01KK0S458A62TV1KJW8KEEA9YZ]** (architecture/critical, score: 0.97, candidate)
  Don't derive status flags (e.g. 'reviewed') from fields that other actors populate (e.g. athlete-submitted scores). Use a dedicated reviewedAt column instead.

file.content:
---
status: proposed
date: 2025-12-22
decision-makers: [Ian Jones]
consulted: []
informed: []
---

# ADR-0001: Migrate from Next.js to TanStack Start on Cloudflare

## Context and Problem Statement

The WodApp is currently built on Next.js 15 with the App Router, deployed to Cloudflare Workers via OpenNext. While this stack works, we face several pain points:

1. **OpenNext complexity**: We rely on a third-party adapter (OpenNext) to deploy Next.js to Cloudflare, which adds a layer of abstraction and potential compatibility issues
2. **Type safety gaps**: Route parameters and search params require manual validation; there's no end-to-end type safety from route definition to component
3. **Server action patterns**: We use ZSA (a custom wrapper) to add middleware and validation to server actions, but this is a custom solution we maintain
4. **Build complexity**: The Next.js + OpenNext + Cloudflare pipeline has multiple moving parts

Should we migrate to TanStack Start, which offers native Cloudflare support and end-to-end type safety?

## Decision Drivers

- **Cloudflare-native deployment** - Eliminate the OpenNext abstraction layer
- **End-to-end type safety** - Type-safe routes, params, search params, and server functions
- **Simpler server function patterns** - Built-in middleware and validation without custom wrappers
- **Future-proofing** - TanStack ecosystem is actively developed and gaining adoption
- **Migration effort** - Must be achievable incrementally without a full rewrite
- **Team familiarity** - Learning curve for new patterns and APIs
- **Production stability** - Framework maturity and community support

## Considered Options

1. **Stay on Next.js + OpenNext** - Continue with current stack
2. **Migrate to TanStack Start** - Full migration to TanStack ecosystem
3. **Migrate to Remix** - Alternative React framework with Cloudflare support
4. **Migrate to SolidStart** - Non-React alternative with excellent performance

## Decision Outcome

1→/**
     2→ * Video Submissions Schema
     3→ *
     4→ * Stores athlete video submissions for online competition events.
     5→ * Athletes can submit a video URL for their workout performance.
     6→ * Includes review status tracking for transparency during the verification process.
     7→ */
     8→
     9→import { createId } from "@paralleldrive/cuid2"
    10→import { relations } from "drizzle-orm"
    11→import {
    12→	datetime,
    13→	index,
    14→	mysqlTable,
    15→	text,
    16→	uniqueIndex,
    17→	varchar,
    18→} from "drizzle-orm/mysql-core"
    19→import { commonColumns } from "./common"
    20→import { competitionRegistrationsTable } from "./competitions"
    21→import { userTable } from "./users"
    22→
    23→/**
    24→ * Review status values for video submissions
    25→ * - pending: Submitted and awaiting review
    26→ * - under_review: Currently being reviewed by an organizer
    27→ * - verified: Score has been confirmed as correct
    28→ * - adjusted: Score was modified during review
    29→ * - penalized: Penalties were applied to the submission
    30→ */
    31→export const reviewStatuses = [
    32→	"pending",
    33→	"under_review",
    34→	"verified",
    35→	"adjusted",
    36→	"penalized",
    37→] as const
    38→export type ReviewStatus = (typeof reviewStatuses)[number]
    39→
    40→// ID generator
    41→export const createVideoSubmissionId = () => `vsub_${createId()}`
    42→
    43→/**
    44→ * Video Submissions Table
    45→ *
    46→ * Stores video submissions from athletes for online competition events.
    47→ * Each athlete can only have one video submission per event.
    48→ */
    49→export const videoSubmissionsTable = mysqlTable(
    50→	"video_submissions",
    51→	{
    52→		...commonColumns,
    53→		id: varchar({ length: 255 })
    54→			.primaryKey()
    55→			.$defaultFn(createVideoSubmissionId),
    56→
    57→		// The registration this submission belongs to
    58→		registrationId: varchar({ length: 255 }).notNull(),
    59→
    60→		// The competition event (track workout) this submission is for
    61→		// Uses trackWorkoutId to match the competitionEventsTable pattern
    62→		trackWorkoutId: varchar({ length: 255 }).notNull(),
    63→
    64→		// The user who submitted the video
    65→		userId: varchar({ length: 255 }).notNull(),
    66→
    67→		// The video URL (typically YouTube, but can be other platforms)
    68→		videoUrl: varchar({ length: 2000 }).notNull(),
    69→
    70→		// Optional notes from the athlete
    71→		notes: text(),
    72→
    73→		// When the video was submitted
    74→		submittedAt: datetime().notNull(),
    75→
    76→		// Review status tracking for transparency
    77→		// Defaults to "pending" when submission is created
    78→		reviewStatus: varchar("review_status", { length: 50 })
    79→			.notNull()
    80→			.default("pending"),
    81→
    82→		// When the review status was last updated
    83→		// Null until status changes from initial pending state
    84→		statusUpdatedAt: datetime(),
    85→
    86→		// Optional notes from the reviewer explaining status changes
    87→		reviewerNotes: text("reviewer_notes"),
    88→
    89→		// When an organizer reviewed this submission (null = pending)
    90→		reviewedAt: datetime(),
    91→
    92→		// The organizer who reviewed this submission
    93→		reviewedBy: varchar({ length: 255 }),
    94→	},
    95→	(table) => [
    96→		// One video submission per registration per event
    97→		uniqueIndex("video_submissions_reg_event_idx").on(
    98→			table.registrationId,
    99→			table.trackWorkoutId,
   100→		),
   101→		// Lookup by user
   102→		index("video_submissions_user_idx").on(table.userId),
   103→		// Lookup by event
   104→		index("video_submissions_event_idx").on(table.trackWorkoutId),
   105→		// Lookup by registration
   106→		index("video_submissions_registration_idx").on(table.registrationId),
   107→	],
   108→)
   109→
   110→// Relations
   111→export const videoSubmissionsRelations = relations(
   112→	videoSubmissionsTable,
   113→	({ one }) => ({
   114→		registration: one(competitionRegistrationsTable, {
   115→			fields: [videoSubmissionsTable.registrationId],
   116→			references: [competitionRegistrationsTable.id],
   117→		}),
   118→		user: one(userTable, {
   119→			fields: [videoSubmissionsTable.userId],
   120→			references: [userTable.id],
   121→		}),
   122→	}),
   123→)
   124→
   125→// Type exports
   126→export type VideoSubmission = typeof videoSubmissionsTable.$inferSelect
   127→export type VideoSubmissionInsert = typeof videoSubmissionsTable.$inferInsert
   128→

1→/**
     2→ * Scores Schema
     3→ *
     4→ * A new unified scoring system that replaces the results + sets tables.
     5→ * Designed for efficient sorting and querying.
     6→ */
     7→
     8→import { createId } from "@paralleldrive/cuid2"
     9→import { relations } from "drizzle-orm"
    10→import {
    11→	boolean,
    12→	datetime,
    13→	index,
    14→	int,
    15→	mysqlTable,
    16→	text,
    17→	uniqueIndex,
    18→	varchar,
    19→} from "drizzle-orm/mysql-core"
    20→import { commonColumns } from "./common"
    21→import { scalingLevelsTable } from "./scaling"
    22→import { teamTable } from "./teams"
    23→import { userTable } from "./users"
    24→import { workouts } from "./workouts"
    25→
    26→// ID generators
    27→export const createScoreId = () => `score_${createId()}`
    28→export const createScoreRoundId = () => `scrd_${createId()}`
    29→export const createScoreVerificationLogId = () => `svlog_${createId()}`
    30→
    31→// Score status values for the new scores table
    32→// This is a subset focused on competition/scoring context
    33→const SCORE_STATUS_NEW_VALUES = ["scored", "cap", "dq", "withdrawn"] as const
    34→export type ScoreStatusNew = (typeof SCORE_STATUS_NEW_VALUES)[number]
    35→
    36→/**
    37→ * Core scores table
    38→ *
    39→ * Stores workout results with encoded integer values for efficient sorting.
    40→ * Replaces the old results + sets tables.
    41→ */
    42→export const scoresTable = mysqlTable(
    43→	"scores",
    44→	{
    45→		...commonColumns,
    46→		id: varchar({ length: 255 }).primaryKey().$defaultFn(createScoreId),
    47→
    48→		// Ownership
    49→		userId: varchar({ length: 255 }).notNull(),
    50→		teamId: varchar({ length: 255 }).notNull(),
    51→
    52→		// What was scored
    53→		workoutId: varchar({ length: 255 }).notNull(),
    54→		competitionEventId: varchar({ length: 255 }), // NULL for personal logs
    55→		scheduledWorkoutInstanceId: varchar({ length: 255 }),
    56→
    57→		// Score classification
    58→		scheme: varchar({ length: 255 }).notNull(),
    59→		scoreType: varchar({ length: 255 }).notNull().default("max"),
    60→
    61→		// Primary score (encoded as integer based on scheme)
    62→		// Time: milliseconds, Rounds+Reps: rounds*100000+reps, Load: grams, Distance: mm
    63→		scoreValue: int(),
    64→
    65→		// Tiebreak
    66→		tiebreakScheme: varchar({ length: 255 }),
    67→		tiebreakValue: int(),
    68→
    69→		// Time cap handling (for time-with-cap workouts)
    70→		timeCapMs: int(),
    71→		// Note: secondaryScheme removed - when capped, score is always reps
    72→		secondaryValue: int(), // reps completed if capped
    73→
    74→		// Status & sorting
    75→		status: varchar({ length: 255 }).notNull().default("scored"),
    76→		statusOrder: int().notNull().default(0), // 0=scored, 1=cap, 2=dq, 3=withdrawn
    77→		// Compound sort key: encodes status + normalized score for single-column sorting
    78→		sortKey: varchar({ length: 255 }),
    79→
    80→		// Scaling
    81→		scalingLevelId: varchar({ length: 255 }),
    82→		asRx: boolean().notNull().default(false),
    83→
    84→		// Metadata
    85→		notes: text(),
    86→		// When the workout was performed (Unix timestamp ms)
    87→		recordedAt: datetime().notNull(),
    88→
    89→		// Verification (organizer review for online competitions)
    90→		// null = unreviewed, "verified" = confirmed, "adjusted" = overridden
    91→		verificationStatus: varchar({ length: 20 }),
    92→		verifiedAt: datetime(),
    93→		verifiedByUserId: varchar({ length: 255 }),
    94→	},
    95→	(table) => [
    96→		// User's scores, ordered by date
    97→		index("idx_scores_user").on(table.userId, table.recordedAt),
    98→		// Leaderboard queries: workout + team, sorted by status and score
    99→		index("idx_scores_workout").on(
   100→			table.workoutId,
   101→			table.teamId,
   102→			table.statusOrder,
   103→			table.sortKey,
   104→		),
   105→		// Competition leaderboards
   106→		index("idx_scores_competition").on(
   107→			table.competitionEventId,
   108→			table.statusOrder,
   109→			table.sortKey,
   110→		),
   111→		// Scheduled workout lookup
   112→		index("idx_scores_scheduled").on(table.scheduledWorkoutInstanceId),
   113→		// Unique constraint for competition scores (one score per user per event)
   114→		uniqueIndex("idx_scores_competition_user_unique").on(
   115→			table.competitionEventId,
   116→			table.userId,
   117→		),
   118→	],
   119→)
   120→
   121→/**
   122→ * Score rounds table
   123→ *
   124→ * Stores individual rounds/sets within a score.
   125→ * For multi-round workouts like "10x3 Back Squat" or "3 rounds for time".
   126→ */
   127→export const scoreRoundsTable = mysqlTable(
   128→	"score_rounds",
   129→	{
   130→		id: varchar({ length: 255 }).primaryKey().$defaultFn(createScoreRoundId),
   131→		scoreId: varchar({ length: 255 }).notNull(),
   132→
   133→		// Round ordering (1-indexed)
   134→		roundNumber: int().notNull(),
   135→
   136→		// The value for this round (encoded based on parent score's scheme)
   137→		value: int().notNull(),
   138→
   139→		// Optional: different scheme per round (rare, but possible)
   140→		schemeOverride: varchar({ length: 255 }),
   141→
   142→		// Status for this specific round
   143→		status: varchar({ length: 255 }),
   144→
   145→		// For time-capped rounds
   146→		secondaryValue: int(),
   147→
   148→		// Metadata
   149→		notes: text(),
   150→		createdAt: datetime()
   151→			.$defaultFn(() => new Date())
   152→			.notNull(),
   153→	},
   154→	(table) => [
   155→		// Lookup rounds for a score
   156→		index("idx_score_rounds_score").on(table.scoreId, table.roundNumber),
   157→		// Unique constraint on score + round number
   158→		uniqueIndex("idx_score_rounds_unique").on(table.scoreId, table.roundNumber),
   159→	],
   160→)
   161→
   162→// Relations
   163→export const scoresRelations = relations(scoresTable, ({ one, many }) => ({
   164→	user: one(userTable, {
   165→		fields: [scoresTable.userId],
   166→		references: [userTable.id],
   167→	}),
   168→	team: one(teamTable, {
   169→		fields: [scoresTable.teamId],
   170→		references: [teamTable.id],
   171→	}),
   172→	workout: one(workouts, {
   173→		fields: [scoresTable.workoutId],
   174→		references: [workouts.id],
   175→	}),
   176→	scalingLevel: one(scalingLevelsTable, {
   177→		fields: [scoresTable.scalingLevelId],
   178→		references: [scalingLevelsTable.id],
   179→	}),
   180→	rounds: many(scoreRoundsTable),
   181→}))
   182→
   183→export const scoreRoundsRelations = relations(scoreRoundsTable, ({ one }) => ({
   184→	score: one(scoresTable, {
   185→		fields: [scoreRoundsTable.scoreId],
   186→		references: [scoresTable.id],
   187→	}),
   188→}))
   189→
   190→/**
   191→ * Score verification log table
   192→ *
   193→ * Append-only audit trail of every organizer verify/adjust action on a score.
   194→ * For "adjusted" entries, captures the original values before overwrite so
   195→ * the athlete's claimed score is never permanently lost.
   196→ */
   197→export const scoreVerificationLogsTable = mysqlTable(
   198→	"score_verification_logs",
   199→	{
   200→		id: varchar({ length: 255 })
   201→			.primaryKey()
   202→			.$defaultFn(createScoreVerificationLogId),
   203→
   204→		// The score that was acted on
   205→		scoreId: varchar({ length: 255 }).notNull(),
   206→
   207→		// Competition context (denormalized for easy querying, null for non-competition scores)
   208→		competitionId: varchar({ length: 255 }),
   209→		trackWorkoutId: varchar({ length: 255 }),
   210→
   211→		// The athlete who owns the score
   212→		athleteUserId: varchar({ length: 255 }).notNull(),
   213→
   214→		// What the organizer did
   215→		action: varchar({ length: 20 }).notNull(), // "verified" | "adjusted"
   216→
   217→		// Values before adjustment (null for "verified" action)
   218→		originalScoreValue: int(),
   219→		originalStatus: varchar({ length: 50 }),
   220→		originalSecondaryValue: int(),
   221→		originalTiebreakValue: int(),
   222→
   223→		// Values after adjustment (null for "verified" action)
   224→		newScoreValue: int(),
   225→		newStatus: varchar({ length: 50 }),
   226→		newSecondaryValue: int(),
   227→		newTiebreakValue: int(),
   228→
   229→		// Who did it and when
   230→		performedByUserId: varchar({ length: 255 }).notNull(),
   231→		performedAt: datetime().notNull(),
   232→	},
   233→	(table) => [
   234→		// Look up all log entries for a score
   235→		index("idx_svlog_score").on(table.scoreId),
   236→		// Look up all actions by an organizer
   237→		index("idx_svlog_performer").on(table.performedByUserId, table.performedAt),
   238→	],
   239→)
   240→
   241→export const scoreVerificationLogsRelations = relations(
   242→	scoreVerificationLogsTable,
   243→	({ one }) => ({
   244→		score: one(scoresTable, {
   245→			fields: [scoreVerificationLogsTable.scoreId],
   246→			references: [scoresTable.id],
   247→		}),
   248→		performedBy: one(userTable, {
   249→			fields: [scoreVerificationLogsTable.performedByUserId],
   250→			references: [userTable.id],
   251→		}),
   252→	}),
   253→)
   254→
   255→// Type exports
   256→export type Score = typeof scoresTable.$inferSelect
   257→export type ScoreInsert = typeof scoresTable.$inferInsert
   258→export type ScoreRound = typeof scoreRoundsTable.$inferSelect
   259→export type ScoreRoundInsert = typeof scoreRoundsTable.$inferInsert
   260→

1→/**
     2→ * Submission Verification Server Functions
     3→ *
     4→ * Server functions for organizers to view and verify athlete video submissions
     5→ * for online competition events.
     6→ */
     7→
     8→import { createServerFn } from "@tanstack/react-start"
     9→import { and, desc, eq, inArray } from "drizzle-orm"
    10→import { z } from "zod"
    11→import { getDb } from "@/db"
    12→import {
    13→	competitionEventsTable,
    14→	competitionRegistrationsTable,
    15→	competitionsTable,
    16→} from "@/db/schemas/competitions"
    17→import { trackWorkoutsTable } from "@/db/schemas/programming"
    18→import { scalingLevelsTable } from "@/db/schemas/scaling"
    19→import { scoreVerificationLogsTable, scoresTable } from "@/db/schemas/scores"
    20→import { TEAM_PERMISSIONS } from "@/db/schemas/teams"
    21→import { userTable } from "@/db/schemas/users"
    22→import { videoSubmissionsTable } from "@/db/schemas/video-submissions"
    23→import { workouts } from "@/db/schemas/workouts"
    24→import { logInfo } from "@/lib/logging"
    25→import {
    26→	computeSortKey,
    27→	decodeScore,
    28→	encodeScore,
    29→	sortKeyToString,
    30→	type WorkoutScheme,
    31→} from "@/lib/scoring"
    32→import { getSessionFromCookie } from "@/utils/auth"
    33→import { autochunk } from "@/utils/batch-query"
    34→import { requireTeamPermission } from "@/utils/team-auth"
    35→
    36→// ============================================================================
    37→// Types
    38→// ============================================================================
    39→
    40→export interface VerificationInfo {
    41→	status: "verified" | "adjusted" | null
    42→	verifiedAt: Date | null
    43→	verifiedByName: string | null
    44→}
    45→
    46→export interface SubmissionDetail {
    47→	id: string
    48→	athlete: {
    49→		userId: string
    50→		firstName: string
    51→		lastName: string
    52→		email: string
    53→		avatar: string | null
    54→		divisionId: string | null
    55→		divisionLabel: string
    56→		teamName: string | null
    57→		registrationId: string
    58→	}
    59→	score: {
    60→		displayValue: string
    61→		rawValue: number | null
    62→		status: string
    63→		tiebreakValue: string | null
    64→		secondaryValue: number | null
    65→	}
    66→	verification: VerificationInfo
    67→	videoUrl: string | null
    68→	submittedAt: Date
    69→	notes: string | null
    70→}
    71→
    72→export interface EventDetails {
    73→	id: string
    74→	trackOrder: number
    75→	workout: {
    76→		id: string
    77→		name: string
    78→		description: string
    79→		scheme: string
    80→		timeCap: number | null
    81→	}
    82→	submissionWindow: {
    83→		opensAt: string | null
    84→		closesAt: string | null
    85→	}
    86→}
    87→
    88→export interface SubmissionListItem {
    89→	id: string
    90→	athleteName: string
    91→	teamName: string | null
    92→	divisionLabel: string
    93→	hasVideo: boolean
    94→	scoreDisplay: string
    95→	status: string
    96→}
    97→
    98→// ============================================================================
    99→// Input Schemas
   100→// ============================================================================
   101→
   102→const getSubmissionDetailInputSchema = z.object({
   103→	competitionId: z.string().min(1),
   104→	trackWorkoutId: z.string().min(1),
   105→	scoreId: z.string().min(1),
   106→})
   107→
   108→const getEventSubmissionsInputSchema = z.object({
   109→	competitionId: z.string().min(1),
   110→	trackWorkoutId: z.string().min(1),
   111→})
   112→
   113→const getEventDetailsForVerificationInputSchema = z.object({
   114→	competitionId: z.string().min(1),
   115→	trackWorkoutId: z.string().min(1),
   116→})
   117→
   118→const getVerificationLogsInputSchema = z.object({
   119→	scoreId: z.string().min(1),
   120→	competitionId: z.string().min(1),
   121→})
   122→
   123→export interface VerificationLogEntry {
   124→	id: string
   125→	action: string
   126→	performedByName: string
   127→	performedAt: Date
   128→	originalScoreValue: number | null
   129→	originalStatus: string | null
   130→	newScoreValue: number | null
   131→	newStatus: string | null
   132→	scheme: string | null
   133→}
   134→
   135→const verifySubmissionScoreInputSchema = z.object({
   136→	competitionId: z.string().min(1),
   137→	trackWorkoutId: z.string().min(1),
   138→	scoreId: z.string().min(1),
   139→	action: z.enum(["verify", "adjust"]),
   140→	// Required only when action === "adjust"
   141→	adjustedScore: z.string().optional(),
   142→	adjustedScoreStatus: z.enum(["scored", "cap"]).optional(),
   143→	secondaryScore: z.string().optional(),
   144→	tieBreakScore: z.string().optional(),
   145→	reviewerNotes: z.string().optional(),
   146→})
   147→
   148→// ============================================================================
   149→// Server Functions
   150→// ============================================================================
   151→
   152→/**
   153→ * Verify or adjust a submitted competition score
   154→ */
   155→export const verifySubmissionScoreFn = createServerFn({ method: "POST" })
   156→	.inputValidator((data: unknown) =>
   157→		verifySubmissionScoreInputSchema.parse(data),
   158→	)
   159→	.handler(
   160→		async ({
   161→			data,
   162→		}): Promise<{ success: boolean; verificationStatus: string }> => {
   163→			const db = getDb()
   164→
   165→			const session = await getSessionFromCookie()
   166→			if (!session?.userId) {
   167→				throw new Error("Not authenticated")
   168→			}
   169→
   170→			// Load competition to get organizingTeamId
   171→			const [competition] = await db
   172→				.select({ organizingTeamId: competitionsTable.organizingTeamId })
   173→				.from(competitionsTable)
   174→				.where(eq(competitionsTable.id, data.competitionId))
   175→				.limit(1)
   176→
   177→			if (!competition) {
   178→				throw new Error("Competition not found")
   179→			}
   180→
   181→			await requireTeamPermission(
   182→				competition.organizingTeamId,
   183→				TEAM_PERMISSIONS.MANAGE_COMPETITIONS,
   184→			)
   185→
   186→			// Verify the event belongs to this competition
   187→			const [competitionEvent] = await db
   188→				.select({ id: competitionEventsTable.id })
   189→				.from(competitionEventsTable)
   190→				.where(
   191→					and(
   192→						eq(competitionEventsTable.competitionId, data.competitionId),
   193→						eq(competitionEventsTable.trackWorkoutId, data.trackWorkoutId),
   194→					),
   195→				)
   196→				.limit(1)
   197→
   198→			if (!competitionEvent) {
   199→				throw new Error("Event not found in this competition")
   200→			}
   201→
   202→			// Load the score (including current values for audit log)
   203→			const [score] = await db
   204→				.select({
   205→					id: scoresTable.id,
   206→					userId: scoresTable.userId,
   207→					scheme: scoresTable.scheme,
   208→					scoreType: scoresTable.scoreType,
   209→					tiebreakScheme: scoresTable.tiebreakScheme,
   210→					timeCapMs: scoresTable.timeCapMs,
   211→					scoreValue: scoresTable.scoreValue,
   212→					status: scoresTable.status,
   213→					secondaryValue: scoresTable.secondaryValue,
   214→					tiebreakValue: scoresTable.tiebreakValue,
   215→				})
   216→				.from(scoresTable)
   217→				.where(
   218→					and(
   219→						eq(scoresTable.id, data.scoreId),
   220→						eq(scoresTable.competitionEventId, data.trackWorkoutId),
   221→					),
   222→				)
   223→				.limit(1)
   224→
   225→			if (!score) {
   226→				throw new Error("Score not found")
   227→			}
   228→
   229→			// Look up the registration for this user in this competition
   230→			// so we can scope video submission updates correctly
   231→			const [registration] = await db
   232→				.select({ id: competitionRegistrationsTable.id })
   233→				.from(competitionRegistrationsTable)
   234→				.where(
   235→					and(
   236→						eq(competitionRegistrationsTable.eventId, data.competitionId),
   237→						eq(competitionRegistrationsTable.userId, score.userId),
   238→					),
   239→				)
   240→				.limit(1)
   241→
   242→			const now = new Date()
   243→
   244→			if (data.action === "verify") {
   245→				await db.transaction(async (tx) => {
   246→					await tx
   247→						.update(scoresTable)
   248→						.set({
   249→							verificationStatus: "verified",
   250→							verifiedAt: now,
   251→							verifiedByUserId: session.userId,
   252→							updatedAt: now,
   253→						})
   254→						.where(eq(scoresTable.id, data.scoreId))
   255→
   256→					await tx.insert(scoreVerificationLogsTable).values({
   257→						scoreId: data.scoreId,
   258→						competitionId: data.competitionId,
   259→						trackWorkoutId: data.trackWorkoutId,
   260→						athleteUserId: score.userId,
   261→						action: "verified",
   262→						performedByUserId: session.userId,
   263→						performedAt: now,
   264→					})
   265→
   266→					// Update the corresponding video submission's review status
   267→					if (registration) {
   268→						await tx
   269→							.update(videoSubmissionsTable)
   270→							.set({
   271→								reviewStatus: "verified",
   272→								statusUpdatedAt: now,
   273→								reviewedAt: now,
   274→								reviewedBy: session.userId,
   275→								reviewerNotes: data.reviewerNotes || null,
   276→							})
   277→							.where(
   278→								and(
   279→									eq(videoSubmissionsTable.registrationId, registration.id),
   280→									eq(
   281→										videoSubmissionsTable.trackWorkoutId,
   282→										data.trackWorkoutId,
   283→									),
   284→								),
   285→							)
   286→					}
   287→				})
   288→
   289→				logInfo({
   290→					message: "[Score] Organizer verified score",
   291→					attributes: {
   292→						scoreId: data.scoreId,
   293→						competitionId: data.competitionId,
   294→						verifiedByUserId: session.userId,
   295→					},
   296→				})
   297→
   298→				return { success: true, verificationStatus: "verified" }
   299→			}
   300→
   301→			// action === "adjust"
   302→			if (!data.adjustedScore || !data.adjustedScoreStatus) {
   303→				throw new Error(
   304→					"adjustedScore and adjustedScoreStatus are required for adjust action",
   305→				)
   306→			}
   307→
   308→			const scheme = score.scheme as WorkoutScheme
   309→			const newStatus = data.adjustedScoreStatus
   310→
   311→			// Encode the adjusted score
   312→			let encodedValue: number | null = null
   313→			if (newStatus === "cap" && score.timeCapMs) {
   314→				encodedValue = score.timeCapMs
   315→			} else {
   316→				encodedValue = encodeScore(data.adjustedScore, scheme)
   317→			}
   318→
   319→			// Parse secondary value (reps at cap)
   320→			let secondaryValue: number | null = null
   321→			if (data.secondaryScore && newStatus === "cap") {
   322→				const parsed = Number.parseInt(data.secondaryScore.trim(), 10)
   323→				if (!Number.isNaN(parsed) && parsed >= 0) {
   324→					secondaryValue = parsed
   325→				}
   326→			}
   327→
   328→			// Encode tiebreak if provided
   329→			let tiebreakValue: number | null = null
   330→			if (data.tieBreakScore && score.tiebreakScheme) {
   331→				try {
   332→					tiebreakValue = encodeScore(
   333→						data.tieBreakScore,
   334→						score.tiebreakScheme as WorkoutScheme,
   335→					)
   336→				} catch {
   337→					// Ignore tiebreak encoding errors
   338→				}
   339→			}
   340→
   341→			// Compute sort key
   342→			const statusOrder = newStatus === "cap" ? 1 : 0
   343→			const sortKey =
   344→				encodedValue !== null
   345→					? computeSortKey({
   346→							value: encodedValue,
   347→							status: newStatus,
   348→							scheme,
   349→							scoreType: (score.scoreType as "max" | "min") ?? "max",
   350→							timeCap:
   351→								newStatus === "cap" &&
   352→								score.timeCapMs &&
   353→								secondaryValue !== null
   354→									? { ms: score.timeCapMs, secondaryValue }
   355→									: undefined,
   356→							tiebreak:
   357→								tiebreakValue !== null && score.tiebreakScheme
   358→									? {
   359→											scheme: score.tiebreakScheme as "time" | "reps",
   360→											value: tiebreakValue,
   361→										}
   362→									: undefined,
   363→						})
   364→					: null
   365→
   366→			await db.transaction(async (tx) => {
   367→				await tx
   368→					.update(scoresTable)
   369→					.set({
   370→						scoreValue: encodedValue,
   371→						status: newStatus,
   372→						statusOrder,
   373→						sortKey: sortKey ? sortKeyToString(sortKey) : null,
   374→						secondaryValue,
   375→						tiebreakValue,
   376→						verificationStatus: "adjusted",
   377→						verifiedAt: now,
   378→						verifiedByUserId: session.userId,
   379→						updatedAt: now,
   380→					})
   381→					.where(eq(scoresTable.id, data.scoreId))
   382→
   383→				await tx.insert(scoreVerificationLogsTable).values({
   384→					scoreId: data.scoreId,
   385→					competitionId: data.competitionId,
   386→					trackWorkoutId: data.trackWorkoutId,
   387→					athleteUserId: score.userId,
   388→					action: "adjusted",
   389→					originalScoreValue: score.scoreValue,
   390→					originalStatus: score.status,
   391→					originalSecondaryValue: score.secondaryValue,
   392→					originalTiebreakValue: score.tiebreakValue,
   393→					newScoreValue: encodedValue,
   394→					newStatus,
   395→					newSecondaryValue: secondaryValue,
   396→					newTiebreakValue: tiebreakValue,
   397→					performedByUserId: session.userId,
   398→					performedAt: now,
   399→				})
   400→
   401→				// Update the corresponding video submission's review status
   402→				if (registration) {
   403→					await tx
   404→						.update(videoSubmissionsTable)
   405→						.set({
   406→							reviewStatus: "adjusted",
   407→							statusUpdatedAt: now,
   408→							reviewedAt: now,
   409→							reviewedBy: session.userId,
   410→							reviewerNotes: data.reviewerNotes || null,
   411→						})
   412→						.where(
   413→							and(
   414→								eq(videoSubmissionsTable.registrationId, registration.id),
   415→								eq(videoSubmissionsTable.trackWorkoutId, data.trackWorkoutId),
   416→							),
   417→						)
   418→				}
   419→			})
   420→
   421→			logInfo({
   422→				message: "[Score] Organizer adjusted score",
   423→				attributes: {
   424→					scoreId: data.scoreId,
   425→					competitionId: data.competitionId,
   426→					verifiedByUserId: session.userId,
   427→					newStatus,
   428→					encodedValue,
   429→				},
   430→			})
   431→
   432→			return { success: true, verificationStatus: "adjusted" }
   433→		},
   434→	)
   435→
   436→/**
   437→ * Get a single submission detail for verification
   438→ */
   439→export const getSubmissionDetailFn = createServerFn({ method: "GET" })
   440→	.inputValidator((data: unknown) => getSubmissionDetailInputSchema.parse(data))
   441→	.handler(
   442→		async ({
   443→			data,
   444→		}): Promise<{
   445→			submission: SubmissionDetail | null
   446→			event: EventDetails
   447→		}> => {
   448→			const db = getDb()
   449→
   450→			const session = await getSessionFromCookie()
   451→			if (!session?.userId) {
   452→				throw new Error("Not authenticated")
   453→			}
   454→
   455→			const [competition] = await db
   456→				.select({ organizingTeamId: competitionsTable.organizingTeamId })
   457→				.from(competitionsTable)
   458→				.where(eq(competitionsTable.id, data.competitionId))
   459→				.limit(1)
   460→
   461→			if (!competition) {
   462→				throw new Error("Competition not found")
   463→			}
   464→
   465→			await requireTeamPermission(
   466→				competition.organizingTeamId,
   467→				TEAM_PERMISSIONS.MANAGE_COMPETITIONS,
   468→			)
   469→
   470→			// Verify the event belongs to this competition and get submission window
   471→			const [competitionEvent] = await db
   472→				.select({
   473→					id: competitionEventsTable.id,
   474→					submissionOpensAt: competitionEventsTable.submissionOpensAt,
   475→					submissionClosesAt: competitionEventsTable.submissionClosesAt,
   476→				})
   477→				.from(competitionEventsTable)
   478→				.where(
   479→					and(
   480→						eq(competitionEventsTable.competitionId, data.competitionId),
   481→						eq(competitionEventsTable.trackWorkoutId, data.trackWorkoutId),
   482→					),
   483→				)
   484→				.limit(1)
   485→
   486→			if (!competitionEvent) {
   487→				throw new Error("Event not found in this competition")
   488→			}
   489→
   490→			// Get the score with user info
   491→			const [score] = await db
   492→				.select({
   493→					id: scoresTable.id,
   494→					userId: scoresTable.userId,
   495→					scoreValue: scoresTable.scoreValue,
   496→					status: scoresTable.status,
   497→					scheme: scoresTable.scheme,
   498→					tiebreakValue: scoresTable.tiebreakValue,
   499→					tiebreakScheme: scoresTable.tiebreakScheme,
   500→					secondaryValue: scoresTable.secondaryValue,
   501→					notes: scoresTable.notes,
   502→					recordedAt: scoresTable.recordedAt,
   503→					scalingLevelId: scoresTable.scalingLevelId,
   504→					verificationStatus: scoresTable.verificationStatus,
   505→					verifiedAt: scoresTable.verifiedAt,
   506→					verifiedByUserId: scoresTable.verifiedByUserId,
   507→				})
   508→				.from(scoresTable)
   509→				.where(
   510→					and(
   511→						eq(scoresTable.id, data.scoreId),
   512→						eq(scoresTable.competitionEventId, data.trackWorkoutId),
   513→					),
   514→				)
   515→				.limit(1)
   516→
   517→			// Get the event details
   518→			const [trackWorkout] = await db
   519→				.select({
   520→					id: trackWorkoutsTable.id,
   521→					trackOrder: trackWorkoutsTable.trackOrder,
   522→					workoutId: workouts.id,
   523→					workoutName: workouts.name,
   524→					workoutDescription: workouts.description,
   525→					workoutScheme: workouts.scheme,
   526→					workoutTimeCap: workouts.timeCap,
   527→				})
   528→				.from(trackWorkoutsTable)
   529→				.innerJoin(workouts, eq(trackWorkoutsTable.workoutId, workouts.id))
   530→				.where(eq(trackWorkoutsTable.id, data.trackWorkoutId))
   531→				.limit(1)
   532→
   533→			if (!trackWorkout) {
   534→				throw new Error("Event not found")
   535→			}
   536→
   537→			const event: EventDetails = {
   538→				id: trackWorkout.id,
   539→				trackOrder: trackWorkout.trackOrder,
   540→				workout: {
   541→					id: trackWorkout.workoutId,
   542→					name: trackWorkout.workoutName,
   543→					description: trackWorkout.workoutDescription,
   544→					scheme: trackWorkout.workoutScheme,
   545→					timeCap: trackWorkout.workoutTimeCap,
   546→				},
   547→				submissionWindow: {
   548→					opensAt: competitionEvent?.submissionOpensAt ?? null,
   549→					closesAt: competitionEvent?.submissionClosesAt ?? null,
   550→				},
   551→			}
   552→
   553→			if (!score) {
   554→				return { submission: null, event }
   555→			}
   556→
   557→			// Get user info
   558→			const [user] = await db
   559→				.select({
   560→					id: userTable.id,
   561→					firstName: userTable.firstName,
   562→					lastName: userTable.lastName,
   563→					email: userTable.email,
   564→					avatar: userTable.avatar,
   565→				})
   566→				.from(userTable)
   567→				.where(eq(userTable.id, score.userId))
   568→				.limit(1)
   569→
   570→			if (!user) {
   571→				return { submission: null, event }
   572→			}
   573→
   574→			// Get division info
   575→			let divisionLabel = "Open"
   576→			if (score.scalingLevelId) {
   577→				const [division] = await db
   578→					.select({ label: scalingLevelsTable.label })
   579→					.from(scalingLevelsTable)
   580→					.where(eq(scalingLevelsTable.id, score.scalingLevelId))
   581→					.limit(1)
   582→				if (division) {
   583→					divisionLabel = division.label
   584→				}
   585→			}
   586→
   587→			// Get registration info (for team name)
   588→			const [registration] = await db
   589→				.select({
   590→					id: competitionRegistrationsTable.id,
   591→					teamName: competitionRegistrationsTable.teamName,
   592→				})
   593→				.from(competitionRegistrationsTable)
   594→				.where(
   595→					and(
   596→						eq(competitionRegistrationsTable.eventId, data.competitionId),
   597→						eq(competitionRegistrationsTable.userId, score.userId),
   598→					),
   599→				)
   600→				.limit(1)
   601→
   602→			// Get video submission from video_submissions table
   603→			let videoUrl: string | null = null
   604→			if (registration) {
   605→				const [videoSubmission] = await db
   606→					.select({ videoUrl: videoSubmissionsTable.videoUrl })
   607→					.from(videoSubmissionsTable)
   608→					.where(
   609→						and(
   610→							eq(videoSubmissionsTable.registrationId, registration.id),
   611→							eq(videoSubmissionsTable.trackWorkoutId, data.trackWorkoutId),
   612→						),
   613→					)
   614→					.limit(1)
   615→				if (videoSubmission) {
   616→					videoUrl = videoSubmission.videoUrl
   617→				}
   618→			}
   619→
   620→			// Get verifier name if verified/adjusted
   621→			let verifiedByName: string | null = null
   622→			if (score.verifiedByUserId) {
   623→				const [verifier] = await db
   624→					.select({
   625→						firstName: userTable.firstName,
   626→						lastName: userTable.lastName,
   627→					})
   628→					.from(userTable)
   629→					.where(eq(userTable.id, score.verifiedByUserId))
   630→					.limit(1)
   631→				if (verifier) {
   632→					verifiedByName =
   633→						`${verifier.firstName || ""} ${verifier.lastName || ""}`.trim() ||
   634→						null
   635→				}
   636→			}
   637→
   638→			// Decode score for display
   639→			let displayValue = ""
   640→			if (score.scoreValue !== null) {
   641→				displayValue = decodeScore(
   642→					score.scoreValue,
   643→					score.scheme as WorkoutScheme,
   644→					{ compact: false },
   645→				)
   646→			}
   647→
   648→			// Decode tiebreak if present
   649→			let tiebreakDisplay: string | null = null
   650→			if (score.tiebreakValue !== null && score.tiebreakScheme) {
   651→				tiebreakDisplay = decodeScore(
   652→					score.tiebreakValue,
   653→					score.tiebreakScheme as WorkoutScheme,
   654→					{ compact: false },
   655→				)
   656→			}
   657→
   658→			const submission: SubmissionDetail = {
   659→				id: score.id,
   660→				athlete: {
   661→					userId: user.id,
   662→					firstName: user.firstName || "",
   663→					lastName: user.lastName || "",
   664→					email: user.email || "",
   665→					avatar: user.avatar,
   666→					divisionId: score.scalingLevelId,
   667→					divisionLabel,
   668→					teamName: registration?.teamName ?? null,
   669→					registrationId: registration?.id ?? "",
   670→				},
   671→				score: {
   672→					displayValue,
   673→					rawValue: score.scoreValue,
   674→					status: score.status,
   675→					tiebreakValue: tiebreakDisplay,
   676→					secondaryValue: score.secondaryValue,
   677→				},
   678→				verification: {
   679→					status: (score.verificationStatus as "verified" | "adjusted") ?? null,
   680→					verifiedAt: score.verifiedAt ?? null,
   681→					verifiedByName,
   682→				},
   683→				videoUrl,
   684→				submittedAt: score.recordedAt,
   685→				notes: score.notes,
   686→			}
   687→
   688→			return { submission, event }
   689→		},
   690→	)
